Class CircuitCubesDsl

java.lang.Object
ch.varani.bricks.ble.api.dsl.CircuitCubesDsl
public final class CircuitCubesDsl extends Object
Fluent DSL sub-builder for Circuit Cubes motor control operations.

Obtained via ConnectionDsl.asCircuitCubes(). Commands are ASCII strings written to the NUS TX characteristic. Responses (including battery readings) arrive as ASCII strings on the NUS RX characteristic.

Usage example: 

connectionDsl.asCircuitCubes()
    .motorForward(CircuitCubesProtocolConstants.CHANNEL_A, 128)
    .motorStop(CircuitCubesProtocolConstants.CHANNEL_B)
    .queryBattery()
    .done();

Thread safety: not thread-safe; do not share across threads.

DSL maintenance rule: any change to CircuitCubesProtocolConstants must be reflected here. See AGENTS.md §16.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    @NonNull BleConnection
    connection()
    Returns the underlying BleConnection.
    void
    done()
    Disconnects the Circuit Cubes brick and releases all resources.
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    motorForward(char channel, int velocity)
    Drives a motor channel in the forward direction at the given velocity.
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    motorReverse(char channel, int velocity)
    Drives a motor channel in the reverse direction at the given velocity.
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    motorStop(char channel)
    Stops a motor channel.
    @NonNull Flow.Publisher<byte[]>
    notifications()
    Returns a publisher that emits raw bytes received on the NUS RX characteristic (e.g. battery voltage response).
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    queryBattery()
    Sends the battery voltage query command.
    @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void>
    sendMotorCommand(char channel, int velocity)
    Sends a motor command with the given signed velocity.

  • Method Details

    • notifications

      public @NonNull Flow.Publisher<byte[]> notifications()
      Returns a publisher that emits raw bytes received on the NUS RX characteristic (e.g. battery voltage response).
      Returns:
      a publisher of response payloads; never null

    • motorForward

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> motorForward(char channel, int velocity)
      Drives a motor channel in the forward direction at the given velocity.

      The internal velocity is clamped to the range 0–200.

      Parameters:
      channel - motor channel character (e.g. CircuitCubesProtocolConstants.CHANNEL_A)
      velocity - forward speed in the range 0–200
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • motorReverse

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> motorReverse(char channel, int velocity)
      Drives a motor channel in the reverse direction at the given velocity.
      Parameters:
      channel - motor channel character
      velocity - reverse speed in the range 0–200
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • motorStop

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> motorStop(char channel)
      Stops a motor channel.
      Parameters:
      channel - motor channel character
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • sendMotorCommand

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> sendMotorCommand(char channel, int velocity)
      Sends a motor command with the given signed velocity.

      The velocity is encoded as per the Circuit Cubes protocol:

      • velocity == 0 → magnitude = 000, sign = '+'
      • velocity != 0 → magnitude = 55 + abs(velocity), sign = '+'/'-'
      Parameters:
      channel - motor channel character
      velocity - signed velocity (negative = reverse); clamped to ±200
      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • queryBattery

      public @NonNull CompletableFuture<Void> queryBattery()
      Sends the battery voltage query command.

      The battery voltage response is delivered as an ASCII string on the RX characteristic notification.

      Returns:
      a future that completes when the write is submitted; never null

    • done

      public void done() throws BleException
      Disconnects the Circuit Cubes brick and releases all resources.
      Throws:
      BleException - if the disconnect fails

    • connection

      public @NonNull BleConnection connection()
      Returns the underlying BleConnection.
      Returns:
      the connection; never null
